title: Juicy Details (THM)

headings.text: 1-What tools did the attacker use? (Order by the occurrence in the log)
2- What endpoint was vulnerable to a brute-force attack?
3- What endpoint was vulnerable to SQL injection?
4- What parameter was used for the SQL injection?
5- What endpoint did the attacker try to use to retrieve files? (Include the /)
6- What section of the website did the attacker use to scrape user email addresses?
7- Was their brute-force attack successful? If so, what is the timestamp of the successful login? (Yay/Nay, 11/Apr/2021:09:xx:xx +0000)
8- What user information was the attacker able to retrieve from the endpoint vulnerable to SQL injection?
9- What files did they try to download from the vulnerable endpoint? (endpoint from the previous task, question 5)
10- What service and account name were used to retrieve files from the previous question? (service, username)
11- What service and username were used to gain shell access to the server? (service, username)

overview: You were hired as a SOC Analyst for one of the biggest Juice Shops in the world and an attacker has made their way into your network.

study.overview: You were hired as a SOC Analyst for one of the biggest Juice Shops in the world and an attacker has made their way into your network.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: 1-What tools did the attacker use? (Order by the occurrence in the log)
2- What endpoint was vulnerable to a brute-force attack?
3- What endpoint was vulnerable to SQL injection?
4- What parameter was used for the SQL injection?
5- What endpoint did the attacker try to use to retrieve files? (Include the /)
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.
